header.title: Table
header.subtitle: Semantic HTML table primitive — Table, TableHeader, TableBody, TableRow, TableHead, TableCell. Thin wrappers over standard table elements, styled via nx-table* CSS classes. Use for custom compositions; reach for DataTable when you need sort, pagination, or row selection built-in.

anatomy.title: Anatomy
anatomy.description: Each component maps 1:1 to a standard HTML table element. Compose them in the standard order: TableHeader → TableRow → TableHead; TableBody → TableRow × N → TableCell.

basic.title: Basic table
basic.description: Raw composition of Table primitives. No sorting, no pagination — just markup.

badges.title: With status badges
badges.description: TableCell accepts any React content. Drop a Badge inside for status columns.

actions.title: With actions column
actions.description: Last column with an icon button per row. The header cell is left empty — no label for the actions column is convention.

numbers.title: Right-aligned numbers
numbers.description: Pass className to TableHead and TableCell to align numeric columns to the right.

avatars.title: With avatars
avatars.description: First column with Avatar + name. Standard pattern for user lists.

usage.title: Table vs DataTable
tableWhen: You need full control over the markup structure
Sorting, pagination, and row selection are not needed
You are composing a very specific layout (e.g., a comparison table, a key-value list)
You want to embed the table inside a complex layout that DataTable doesn't accommodate
dataTableWhen: You need built-in sort by column
You need row selection with checkboxes
You need loading skeletons handled automatically
This covers most domain entity lists (users, reports, transactions…)
usage.note: DataTable is documented in the Data category. It is built on top of these Table primitives.

props.title: Props
props.description: Each wrapper accepts the standard HTML attributes of its underlying element plus className for overrides.

anatomy.table: Root element — <table> wrapper with overflow scroll container
anatomy.tableRow: A <tr> row, used in both head and body
anatomy.tableHead: A <th> header cell — uppercase label, muted color
anatomy.tableCell: A <td> data cell — standard padding and primary text color

props.extendsNote: Each wrapper accepts all standard HTML attributes of its underlying element (table / thead / tbody / tr / th / td) plus className for style overrides via cn().
